### Spec
#### Schema
```yaml
cpuOvercommitType: string
description: string
nodeType: string
nodeTypeFlexibility:
  cpus: string
  localSsd: string
  memory: string
region: string
resourceID: string
serverBinding:
  type: string
```

            <p><code>cpuOvercommitType</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. CPU overcommit. Default value: "NONE" Possible values: ["ENABLED", "NONE"].</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. An optional textual description of the resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>nodeType</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Node type to use for nodes group that are created from this template. Only one of nodeTypeFlexibility and nodeType can be specified.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>nodeTypeFlexibility</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Flexible properties for the desired node type. Node groups that use this node template will create nodes of a type that matches these properties. Only one of nodeTypeFlexibility and nodeType can be specified.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>nodeTypeFlexibility.cpus</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Number of virtual CPUs to use.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>nodeTypeFlexibility.localSsd</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Use local SSD.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>nodeTypeFlexibility.memory</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Physical memory available to the node, defined in MB.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>region</code></p>
            <p><i>Required</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Region where nodes using the node template will be created.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>resourceID</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Optional. The name of the resource. Used for creation and acquisition. When unset, the value of `metadata.name` is used as the default.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>serverBinding</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The server binding policy for nodes using this template. Determines where the nodes should restart following a maintenance event.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>serverBinding.type</code></p>
            <p><i>Required*</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. Type of server binding policy. If 'RESTART_NODE_ON_ANY_SERVER',
nodes using this template will restart on any physical server
following a maintenance event.

If 'RESTART_NODE_ON_MINIMAL_SERVER', nodes using this template
will restart on the same physical server following a maintenance
event, instead of being live migrated to or restarted on a new
physical server. This option may be useful if you are using
software licenses tied to the underlying server characteristics
such as physical sockets or cores, to avoid the need for
additional licenses when maintenance occurs. However, VMs on such
nodes will experience outages while maintenance is applied. Possible values: ["RESTART_NODE_ON_ANY_SERVER", "RESTART_NODE_ON_MINIMAL_SERVERS"].</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<p>* Field is required when parent field is specified</p>

## Sample YAML(s)

### Flexible Node Template
```yaml
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeNodeTemplate
metadata:
  name: computenodetemplate-sample-flexible
  labels:
    memory_guarantee: "false"
    desired_workload: "high-cpu"
spec:
  description: Node template for sole tenant nodes running in us-central1, with 96vCPUs and any amount of memory on any machine type.
  region: us-central1
  nodeTypeFlexibility:
    cpus: "96"
    memory: any
```

### Typed Node Template
```yaml
apiVersion: compute.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: ComputeNodeTemplate
metadata:
  name: computenodetemplate-sample-template
  labels:
    memory_guarantee: "true"
    desired_workload: "sustained"
spec:
  description: Node template for sole tenant nodes running in us-central1, with 96vCPUs and 624GB of memory, on n1 machines.
  region: us-central1
  nodeType: n1-node-96-624
```

Note: If you have any trouble with instantiating the resource, refer to <a href="https://docs.cloud.google.com/config-connector/docs/troubleshooting">Troubleshoot Config Connector</a>.
